title: gouldia terminalis var. elongata
intro: Gouldia terminalis var. elongata, also known as the elongated form of the Gouldia terminalis, is a rare plant species endemic to the island of Tahiti in French Polynesia. It has been traditionally used in Polynesian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, due to its rich content of alkaloids, terpenoids, and other bioactive compounds. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Gouldia terminalis var. elongata are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically for therapeutic purposes.
